questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity (has only magnitude). Velocity is a vector quantity (has both magnitude and direction).

questions.text: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.
questions.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity with time. a = (v-u)/t

questions.correctAnswer: Motion in which equal distances are covered in equal intervals of time.
questions.explanation: In uniform motion, the object moves with constant speed. The distance-time graph is a straight line.

questions.text: The SI unit of acceleration is ________.
questions.explanation: Acceleration is measured in meters per second squared (m/s²) in SI units.
